name:
EASTER VIGIL
IN THE HOLY NIGHT

lectionary.gospelTitle: The Women Find the Tomb Empty (Lk 24:1-12)
lectionary.thematicTag: Easter Vigil — In the Holy Night

readings.summary: The Egyptian army pursues the Israelites, Moses parts the waters.
The Lord calls and will make a lasting covenant with you.

readings.citation:
Is 12:2-3, 4, 5-6, R. v.3
You will draw water joyfully from the springs of salvation.
readings.antiphon: You will draw water joyfully from the springs of salvation.

readings.summary: The Lord returns the people to their land.

readings.citation:
Ps 118:1-2, 16-17, 22-23
This is the day the Lord has made; let us rejoice and be glad.
readings.antiphon: This is the day the Lord has made; let us rejoice and be glad.

readings.summary: The women find the tomb empty.

planningNotes: SAT 7 PM — Easter Vigil, all ensembles combined
Procession with Paschal Candle; Exultet; Service of Readings; Baptismal Liturgy; Liturgy of the Eucharist
First Alleluia of Easter sung after Epistle (Rom 6:3-11)
Litany of Saints during procession to font
Neophytes receive Eucharist for first time

massNotes: SAT 7 PM — Easter Vigil, all ensembles combined
LUCERNARIUM — Procession of Candle: Lumen de Lumine (Jacques Berthier/Taizé, adapted Ed Archer, Choral Dir: Tica) [Track 01a]
EXULTET — This Is the Night (Tony Alonso, Choral Dir: Arielle) [Track 01c]
READING 1 (Gen 1:1—2:2) Musical Setting: Genesis Reading for the Great Vigil (Rory Cooney, SATB, Choral Dir: Tica) [Track 02a]
READING 2 (Ex 14:15—15:1) Musical Setting: Exodus Reading Acclamation (Fr. Jerome Kaywell, SATB) [Track 03]
READING 3 (Is 55:1-11) — No musical element during reading
CANTICLE (Is 12:2-3, 4, 5-6, R. v.3): You Will Draw Water (Ed Archer, SATB, BPM 112, Choral Dir: Tica) [Track 04]
GLORIA: Glory to God from Mass of Joy & Peace (Tony Alonso, arr. Dan Houze, BPM 124) [Track 05]
EPISTLE (Rom 6:3-11): The Lord returns the people to their land
PSALM 118: Refrain from Mass of New Beginnings (Clifford Petty, verses adapted by David Lockwood, BPM 138, Choral Dir: Merrick) [Track 06a] — 3 verses: v1 Ps 118:1-2, v2 Ps 118:16-17, v3 Ps 118:22-23
GOSPEL (Lk 24:1-12) — No musical element during reading
Reprise of Gospel Acclamation from Mass of New Beginnings (Clifford Petty, BPM 138, Choral Dir: Merrick) [Track 06a]
HOMILY SONG: You Say (Lauren Daigle, arr. Dan Galbraith, BPM 74, charts in key of F or G) [Track 07]
BAPTISMAL LITURGY — Litany of the Saints (Tony Alonso, SATB, BPM 98) [Track 08]
Baptismal Acclamation: Springs of Water (Clifford Petty, adapted David Lockwood, BPM 120, Choral Dir: Merrick) [Track 09]
Acclamation for Baptisms: Gospel Acclamation from Mass of New Beginnings (Clifford Petty, BPM 138, Choral Dir: Merrick) [Track 10]
Sprinkling with Baptismal Water: Lead Us to the Water (Tom Kendzia & Gary Daigle, Choral Dir: Merrick) [Track 11]
Song for Anointing: Veni Sancte Spiritus (Christopher Walker, Choral Dir: Tica) [Track 13]
OFFERTORY: He Is Not Here (Derek Campbell, BPM 154, Choral Dir: Arielle) [Track 14]
EUCHARISTIC ACCLAMATIONS from Mass of Glory (Bob Hurd, arr. Dan Houze) — Holy Holy BPM 120, We Proclaim Your Death BPM 110, Amen BPM 115 [Track 15]
LORD'S PRAYER: The Echo Our Father (Jerome Andrews, arr. Dan Houze, BPM 145) [Track 16]
FRACTION RITE: Lamb of God from Mass of Glory (Bob Hurd, arr. Dan Houze, BPM 100) [Track 17]
COMMUNION: Put On Christ (Bob Hurd, BPM 160, Choral Dir: Merrick) [Track 18] → Three Days (BPM 82, Choral Dir: Arielle) [Track 19] → I Am the Bread of Life (SAT, BPM 80, Choral Dir: Tica) [Track 20]
MEDITATION: Sunday Is Coming (BPM 80, Choral Dir: Arielle) [Track 21]
SENDING: Hallelujah Chorus (Handel, SATB, BPM 100, Choral Dir: Tica) [Track 22]
